_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to read, interpret, and analyse information in different formats to identify patterns, trends, or to make inferences about places.

_source.w: We are learning to read, interpret, and analyse information in different formats to identify patterns, trends, or to make inferences about places.
y: Interpretation skills help us make sense of the data we collect and see what it is telling us.
Finding patterns in geographical data helps us understand and explain how places work.
Analysing information critically is essential for forming evidence-based conclusions.
eg: Interpreting a population graph to identify whether a place is growing or shrinking.
Analysing satellite images to spot changes in vegetation over time.
Reading a climate graph and identifying seasonal patterns in rainfall and temperature.
Comparing maps from different time periods to identify how land use has changed.
Making inferences about why certain patterns appear — for example, why population clusters near rivers.
Using multiple data sources to build a deeper understanding of a geographical issue.
